'''Moutoa Lance '''"'''Rangi'''"''' Chase''' (born 11 April 1986) is a [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in [[RFL League 1]].

He played at representative level for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] before moving to Britain and playing for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]], and then the [[England national rugby league team|England national team]]. Chase played in the [[National Rugby League]] for Australian clubs [[Wests Tigers]] and [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] before playing in the [[Super League]] for English clubs [[Castleford Tigers]] and [[Salford Red Devils]]. Chase was the 2011 [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal]] winner and was selected in the England squad for the [[2011 Gillette Four Nations]]. On 4 October 2011, Chase was presented with the [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel Award]] whilst playing for [[Castleford Tigers]]. On 4 August 2017 it was announced that he had been suspended a result of a failed drug test from the previous month and on 23 November 2017 the [[UKAD]] confirmed a two-year ban from rugby. On 14 May 2019, it was announced Chase would return to rugby league with [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] until the end of the season.<ref name=bbc>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/42095344|title=Rangi Chase: Widnes Vikings half-back given two-year ban for positive drugs test|work=BBC Sport |date=23 November 2017|access-date=23 November 2017}}</ref>

==Background==
Born in [[Dannevirke]], New Zealand, Chase is of [[Māori people|Māori]] descent and attended [[Te Aute College]] in [[Hawke's Bay Region|Hawke's Bay]], New Zealand, before moving to Australia where he attended Keebra Park State High School in [[Southport, Queensland]]. Chase begun his career with [[Manawatu rugby league team|Manawatu]] in the [[2002 New Zealand rugby league season|2002 North Island Super 6 competition]].<ref name=manawatu>[https://archive.today/20130104142115/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=13524 Wellington and Manawatu Teams] ''rleague.com'', 20 September 2002</ref>

==Playing career==

===National Rugby League===
Chase started his career in the [[National Rugby League]] with Sydney team [[Wests Tigers]], making his début in the [[2006 NRL season]]. At the end of the year, he moved to the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]. He played there for two seasons. 
[[File:Rangi Chase.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Chase playing for the New Zealand Māori in 2008]]
[[File:Rangi Chase 2013 RLWC.jpg|thumb|right|Chase playing for England in 2013]]
Chase was named for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|New Zealand training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-08|access-date=2008-08-08}}</ref> Instead he played for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori team]].

===Super League===
Chase joined English club [[Castleford Tigers]] for 2009's [[Super League XIV]], linking up in the halves with [[Brent Sherwin]].<ref name="Smith keeps his England plans quiet">{{cite web|url=http://www.express.co.uk/posts/view/54745/Smith-keeps-his-England-plans-quiet|title=Smith keeps his England plans quiet|publisher=Daily Express|date=2008-07-31|access-date=2008-07-31}}</ref><ref name="2009 NRL Player Movements">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements|title=2009 NRL Player Movements|publisher=NRL Live|date=2008-10-04|access-date=2008-10-04|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080626165749/http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/|archive-date=26 June 2008|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Chase scored ten tries and made more tackle busts than any other player in Super League, earning him the Castleford Tigers player of the year award. In August 2009, Chase signed a four-year contract with the Tigers, lasting until the end of the 2013 season. He again played for the New Zealand Maori team in 2010.

In 2011, at Southport Magistrate's Court in Queensland, Australia Chase pleaded guilty to a charge of causing Grievous Bodily Harm and was sentenced to two years imprisonment, suspended.<ref name="courier">{{cite news|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/league-player-rangi-chase-pleads-guilty-to-breaking-mans-jaw-in-gold-coast-pub-fight/story-e6frep66-1226112349337|publisher=Courier Mail|title=League player Rangi Chase gets suspended sentence for breaking man's jaw in Gold Coast pub fight|first=Jeremy|last=Pierce|date=11 August 2011|access-date=18 April 2012}}</ref>

Chase was selected to play against the Exiles in Game 1 of the International Origin, this was the first time a player has represented both teams in the Origin, after Chase received Man of the Match in the 2011 game for the Exiles. Chase was selected for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] squad for the [[International Origin Match]] against [[England national rugby league team|England]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]] on 10 June 2011,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|title=Brian McClennan completes Exiles squad to face England fixture|work=BBC Sport|date=4 May 2011|archive-date=13 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160113141331/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|url-status=live}}</ref> Being named man-of-the-match in the Exiles' victory. Despite this, on 3 October 2011, Chase made himself available for selection for the English national side.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://loverugbyleague.com/news_6444-chase-in-england-squad.html|title=Chase in England squad - Love Rugby League|date=3 October 2011|website=loverugbyleague.com|access-date=23 November 2017}}</ref> In 2011 he became the third consecutive Australasian import to win the [[Man of Steel award]] for [[Super League]]'s player of the season.

He was selected by England despite having played against them earlier in the year, drawing criticism from former player [[Garry Schofield]].<ref>{{cite news|last=AAP|author-link=Australian Associated Press|title=English fury over Chase selection|url=http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-sport/english-fury-over-chase-selection-20111004-1l773.html|access-date=4 October 2011|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|date=4 October 2011}}</ref> Chase said that he asked to represent England as he found himself more "at home" in Castleford than New Zealand.

Chase made his début in the warm up test against France where he won the Man of the Match award. His next game was in the 42–4 win against Wales in the [[2011 Four Nations]]. He also played against Australia at [[Wembley Stadium]], against former schoolmate [[Benji Marshall]], when England beat New Zealand 28–6. Chase's cousin [[Isaac Luke]] bent his leg back in a tackle in the win over New Zealand: Luke, who was put on report for the incident by referee [[Matt Cecchin]], later apologised to Chase for the play. Chase was the scrum-half in the 30–8 loss to Australia in the [[2011 Four Nations]] Final.

At the end of 2011 Chase was awarded the [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal]].<ref>{{cite web|title=The Albert Goldthorpe Awards|url=http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour|work=totalrl.com|publisher=League Publications|access-date=16 February 2014|archive-date=26 March 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140326074057/http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour/|url-status=dead}}</ref> Chase was suspended indefinitely after an apparent breach of club discipline on the weekend of 24 June 2012.<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/7843188/Chase-suspended-by-Cas|title=Chase suspended by Cas|work=[[Sky Sports]]|date=26 June 2012|access-date=10 September 2020}}</ref> He also featured in the [[2012 Autumn International Series]] Final against France.

On 11 February 2013, he was charged by the Super League after a high hit on England teammate Zak Hardaker against Leeds. He was given a two-game ban for a grade B offence. Chase signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in September 2013 for a sum believed to be in the region of £115,000 on a four-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/22642890|title=Salford sign eight more players and relaunch as Red Devils|work=BBC Sport |date=5 September 2013|access-date=23 November 2017}}</ref> He made his début in an 18–14 win over the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], but was injured and taken off after 17 minutes. Chase was part of England's [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] squad. He featured in the warm up against Italy which England lost 15–14. Chase was then selected at stand-off for the group games against Australia, Ireland and Fiji. He also featured in the Quarter Final against France. He scored a try against Ireland.

On 22 September 2015, controversy occurred as Chase, alongside teammates [[Harrison Hansen]] and [[Cory Paterson|Corey Paterson]], had their Salford contracts terminated in order to free up salary cap space for the club to sign eight new players.<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/sep/22/salford-rangi-chase-harrison-hansen-shake-up|title=Salford cut losses on Rangi Chase and Harrison Hansen in major shake-up|work=theguardian|date=22 September 2015|access-date=22 September 2015}}</ref>

Chase signed for the then [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] club [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] for the 2016 and 2017 seasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/10078219/leigh-centurions-sign-former-man-of-steel-rangi-chase|title=Leigh Centurions sign former Man of Steel Rangi Chase|website=skysports.com|access-date=23 November 2017}}</ref> Midway through the 2016 Championship season, Chase left the Leigh club to "pursue other options outside of Rugby League." It was later revealed he was battling depression.

On 1 July 2016, it was speculated via Sky Sports that Chase had re-signed with [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. This was confirmed the next day by the club and he will return for the remainder of the 2016 Super League season with the club option to extend the deal at the end of the season.

On 15 May 2017, it was confirmed that Chase had signed a season long loan deal with Super League Rivals, Widnes. The deal was made permanent, when Chase signed for the Widnes club on a two-year deal, signed on 7 July 2017<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40532318|title=Rangi Chase signs permanent Widnes deal and Danny Walker agrees new contract|work=BBC Sport |date=7 July 2017|access-date=23 November 2017}}</ref> In August 2017, Chase was suspended by Widnes after failing a drugs test and on 23 November 2017 the [[UKAD]] confirmed a two-year ban from rugby.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Bower|first1=Aaron|title=Widnes suspend Rangi Chase after positive test for cocaine|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/aug/03/widnes-suspend-rangi-chase-cocaine|website=The Guardian|access-date=6 August 2017|date=3 August 2017}}</ref><ref name=bbc/>

===West Wales Raiders===
On 9 October 2020 it was reported that Chase had signed for the [[West Wales Raiders]] starting in the 2021.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=9 Oct 2020|access-date=30 Dec 2020|title=Rangi Chase joins West Wales Raiders|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rangi-chase-joins-west-wales-raiders/}}</ref>

===Rochdale Hornets===
On 21 June 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Rochdale Hornets|Rochdale]] in the [[RFL League 1]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 Jun 2021|accessdate=21 Jun 2021|title=Rangi Chase finds new club in Rochdale|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rangi-chase-finds-new-club-in-rochdale/}}</ref>
